author: Luke
content:
With these trades I scale them purely based on the underlying key levels and not the contract's percentage. How I personally use the contract's percentage to my advantage so avoid unnecessary losses is once i'm in at least 15-20% ish profit (which if you get a move down right away will automatically happen) I don't want to make it a loss so I won't hold to my stop loss. The only time I will hold to my stop loss is if the trade doesn't work out right away. So like at my entry here you can see I wanted to scale 40% at 659, but it didn't hit it and rejected, and my contracts went to around breakeven. If there was any strength after that 10:17 candle I would've stopped out immediately with a super small loss (pretty much breakeven).
As far as the R:R tool goes on the underlying chart. With 0DTEs you can't really base it off that much because they move way differently. Sometimes on the underlying you'll be up 1:1R but the contracts are up almost 2R. One thing I personally will do is if the contracts move to 40% and i'm not at TP1 yet, i'll scale some no matter what because that's like a 2R on the contracts because on these type of trades you should be losing about 15-20% on your losses.
So for your first trade that was up 30%, if I hadn't hit TP 1 yet then I would for sure have stopped out when the contracts hit breakeven, because you can always look to re-enter, so just save yourself from a loss and reevaluate. For your second trade, if it hadn't hit TP 1 yet and the contracts were at 30%, then I would've stopped out breakeven. How I play it is when the contracts are at 40% (2R for the contracts) and price rejects, i'll sell the contracts at 15-20% in profit no matter what so that it doesn't become a red trade.
Those are just some rules i've made for myself over the past weeks of trading thse, hopefully that helps

author: FraggDieb
content:
The thing is that options do not behave like the underlying asset, but only very similarly. However, we do not trade the underlying asset and use the option merely as leverage but we use the underlying as our „road map“. So just put two buttons together. Buy Market 
Sell market 

Don’t Look on the option chart. Buy when the underlying chart tells you to buy and exit when the underlying chart tells you again (by stop loss or take profit)

author: EeveeWinter
content: Part of it is momentum. Tony and Jack always say “scale on pops! Or scale on strength!” That’s because the contracts are juiced in those moments and they provide a nicer market value. Same goes for when a trade reverses. Let’s say the upside is moving slowly and then all of a sudden some red candles form quickly to your stop loss. You’re now cutting your loss into weakness and you’ll feel it more. Today was a perfect example on that first NVDA trade. When Tony cut it, he had to take a bigger loss because that candle that broke structure was big and fast. Bigger loss.

author: healthiswealth
content: I had this same question as well. I was winning, but the losses were twice as big. What I noticed was that I was not scaling out my wins properly. Also, I didn't have trailers. Having trailers are HUGE in order to maximize a win. There have been trades where Tony and JDub have made more from the trailers than they did at the first and second scale outs. So, when we don't scale out properly and have trailers, losses will be greater than the wins because we don't match Tony/JDub's exact winning formula and we "leave meat on the bone", but we do match exactly the formula when they take a loss.

author: 305 Trader
content:
Here is a really sinple method I use as an options trader for scalping,

a)- What is the max loss per trade I am willing to accept today given my account size?

b)- when the set up is forming, I watch where my TPs are, I take a TP1 and also the ultimate, TP1 to me should be 1:2, the ultimate is whatever it can be. Understand this is a super conservative  (but game changer) approach. 

c) - Here is the science, what is the underlying (real stock) $cents size of the most realistic stop loss (not an arbitrary one)??
d)
Formula: 

 c) x contracts number x 0.5 = a). 

Example : Stop loss measures  25 cts of stock value. 
Max loss per trade is $50
Factor is 0.5
So,
25 x 0.5 = 12.5
$50 / 12.5 = 4 contracts. 

That is your contract size to lose $50 as per your measured stop loss (as long as you exit when it says so). 

then, 

Your RvR  ratio to TP1 is 1:2
So,

You have to measure  and find 50 stock value cents to TP1 to enter the trade.

Notice I measure 1:2 RvR to TP1, yes I know this is conservative, but not really cause you are only interested anyways in a  small stop loss, so provided this is what you look for, a RvR to TP1 of 1:2 is realistic and it will occur often. 

Hope this helps, with this method you will be more focused on the  size of your stop loss rather than % of your account to discard set ups that are not winners in what you need: Lower risk

Unfortunately the answer is it 'depends' - usually on the type of setup, HTFs , market context etc. On Reclaim trades I take less off (for me less is 10% scale) or none at HOD cos those tend to move aggressively in my favor and I don't want to choke off the trade prematurely. On retests, it depends how much the stock has moved already. If the retest is earlier in the session I'll take less off at HOD etc. 

Keep in mind if trading generally smaller size, each con counts - the math tends to work better if  let our trades run - but def need to backtest and look at your live data to find the answer that works best for you!

author: JABARI
content:
Giving yourself 6 months to come to a conclusion like this isn’t realistic. Trading takes time, just like starting any other business or going to university to get a degree. It’s not a get rich quick scheme. I’m not sure what expectations you had coming into trading, but even trading 1-3 times a week already puts you at a bit of a disadvantage when you’re day trading. You need to be attending every trading day to build that momentum.

As for the trades they take, the announce it as soon as they take it, but options move very quickly and put your entry at a disadvantage especially when you’re waiting for someone else to give you a signal through a livestream. On top of that your broker matters as well with how they’re filling your order. in regards to the candles, there are different entry criteria’s they’ve went through extensively and it’s the same one they follow, the difference is they’re way more experienced with candle formations, discretion, and their edge that they will enter sooner than you can think to enter. 
You can always compare your entry time to the livestream they upload and see if it lines up, and I can assure you it will line up. 

Another thing about tony making 25k+ is because of his position size and the way he scales those cons - you’re not able to do that with your account size and psychology at this time unfortunately. It’s better you continue practising with 3 cons and always leave the last one as a runner so you can build experience or you can always paper trade using more cons to practise scaling.

Stay consistent, give yourself more room and time and be optimistic.

Also, not making a move until almost 30 mins after opens really helps me see more clearly after the opening dust has settled.  It's so easy to overpay for premiums that'll come in a little bit if you can wait an extra 15 mins for the setup to happen.

- your 2nd set up, no proper mitigation, just friction and the entry at LOD.

author: Jonathan
content:
I can acknowledge that it sounds risky, but if you believe in our system and what jack and tony preach it works. They have the proof with years if experience. 
I scalp primarily or "Base Hit" to my levels, get in get out. Rinse and repeat, let the probability play out. 

 If I learned more about HTF I would only use it to add my Bias to upside. That Thursdays levels it tapped...i would've used that as an alert on when to get out of my original ticker position which was PLTR. Either way I'm still entering into LTF for high probability moves EVEN if HTF is "Bearish". You ask how I'll be holding longs if HTF is bearish, because this system is meant to work in all conditions. It's so repeatable and simple to do...You break, you retest, and you Sell on high probability closes like HOD/LOD, whole psychological numbers, EOD, etc... 
That's how I do it. That's what I have learned from this system and will continue to evolve with it.

author: Das Wookie
content:
<@1014389514141184074> you asked if everyone understood why the importance of the $686 key level on SPY was so important, and the rejection off of it. That's not what was rejected tho. This might just be you rounding, but the key level for me was 685.73... not $686. Maybe this is a "Yeah but it's close enough" kinda thing... so you don't mean EXACTLY $686, but basically... my key level wasn't there.

The $686 key level was from HOD in the pre-market, but we didn't quite get there before the price rejected off of $685.73 and headed down.

$685.73 was a key level from HOD on Market Open back on the 4th, and we had a hard bounce off of it yesterday in the initial spike in price from the FOMC release. There was a tight consolidation between $685.73 and $686 in pre-market as well as after hours back on the 5th.

A $685.73 key level seems to have been more important based on the price rejection yesterday, but I just figured that was a level where that initial momentum gave out. $686 is an interesting key level, but mostly from Extended Hours trading.

So, not to be pedantic, but the $686 key level, specifically... in regards to THAT trade... well... uhmmmm.... no. Not really.

The $682.40 level would have been the more interesting one to me for that trade, as that was LOD for pre-market, and as I was watching prices fall, that's the key level I would have been watching VERY closely for a rejection and flip. I was trading the AAPL drop at the time, so the price I was watching for was over there. author: DWalt Trades

Webull makes execution feel like mindless work 🙌

author: Hugo Comte
content:
Got it ! my issue was the delta not being accounted, meaning i was basically half the size.

Whats your actual process when you want to place a trade with the right size with a defined risk in $.

Lets say you want to risk 100$?  i would do the following:

- Place a stop where i want to exit on the underlying.
- define the % risk on the underlying, lets say 1$
- Mutiply by my total risk > 100$ > 100 Shares.
- 100 shares > 1 contracts 
- corect size based on Delta 0.5 > 2 contracts

Thatts just so much step in order to define sizing when i you want to be very quick with your entry

author: Jatin
content:
<@1292620429596299409> I am in the same phase myself. Last 2 months have been absolutely fire for me. I started in last June and went through exactly like this chart you sent.
What worked for me, might not work for you or anyone else. It took me a bit long to accept this but you have to find the criteria that works to your risk appetite and the amount of aggressivness in trading you like. All strategies work.
Define the required criteria when you want to enter trades.
Define your entry model.
Define your take profit and risk level before you enter in any trades.
The way to find out all of this is: Backtesting with some criteria and when you see its working in backtesting sessions, forward test it in realtime market hours for a few weeks to prove the system works if you execute mechanically.

author: Angel (az)
content: Sure thing. Yes they teach candle close for entry. So in reality, they trade differently and are able to enter/exit with more discretion because of their many years of experience whereas we need to build an edge, which requires keeping things simple and the same every day so we can repeat it. The most repeatable unit of time is candle closure. You could say 20s to candle close, as long as you stick with that every single trade, that’s fine too. I would say it’s ok whatever you end up choosing with your stop logic but you must pick one and then stick with it, and not let it become true discretion. I see it as we have not yet earned the right to discretion. It’ll come in time, after we prove consistency!

author: 305 Trader
content: Angel, hi. What do you trade? 0DTE or >1DTE? This is the first consideration. Theres is a huge difference in the way to trade. And this is something is not being taught. In order to make it as a 0DTE trader, you have to lose small and exit as soon as the trade is not taking off. Then, observe the PA and if its still in your favor, consider reenter. Also, you need abundant confirmation, which is what <@1325884177291280475> does and teaches. Also <@801169196015943731> and he calls it a “conservative” approach in his videos. What Tony teaches to exit in the closed candle and that “tails do the damage” is in my view as a 0DTE trader only, only to be implemented when you are trading >1DTE. Why? In 0DTE there is bo intrinsic value of the contracts, their value is only extrinsic, which means, they go up (or down) only because of volatility and time decay. The more Out of the money you are, is even worst for options, that is also another consideration. Options are not straightforward. Suggest you become familiar with IV% and its actual pattern during the trading day. Enter only when IV is rising for longs and puts. But this requires, very tight really tight stop losses for 0DTEs.

author: TonyMontana
content:
HOW TO USE UNDERLYING TICKER AS HARD STOP FOR OPTIONS ON IBKR:

1. right click on option position in portfolio
2. select order ticket
3. in the basic tab of the quote panel, choose action (buy or sell), quantity of contracts, and order type (market will execute as take profit or stop loss at market price of option)
4. in the conditional tab, press the add button
5. choose condition Price, next
6. in the underlying text box, type the name of the ticker of the underlying stock (TSLA for example and press enter. this should bring up the security lookup screen. Select Stock (SMART)
7. enter operator and Price. For example, for stop loss, enter the price of the stop and the
operator ＜=
8. press the FINISH button
9. set the logic column to "or"
10. repeat steps 6,7,8,9 entering the take profit price and using the
>= operator
11. Press the "Accept" button in the order ticket quote panel
12. review the trade you just entered in the orders panel to ensure everything is correct
13. press the transmit button

content:
Hey guys!

Want to make one thing clear,

if you are RED today DO NOT be upset.

Look at the price action in the pic below.

That's QQQ.

It did NOTHING.

All range / chop action, every hod/lod came back in the range.

It was a difficult day to trade, the market always has cycles where it's very easy (last 2-3 months) then it will get difficult for 1-2 weeks and then it'll go back.

Once you understand these cycles you'll learn when to be aggressive and defensive OR when to play HTF vs LTF.

With this being said tonight's youtube video will help alot of you guys out that got caught up in the chop.

5PM EST, see y'all then!

(Trade review coming out tn as well)

author: TonyMontana
content:
**$75,000 Week Reflection**

I've never done this but since I didn't take a trade today I wanted to try something new.

When I started trading it was solely for money.

Yes, people will say it's for a noble cause, family, helping people, etc...

No.

We all start trading to make money, this is the reality.

The noble causes we use the money for come after, don't get that confused because I've been able to help many people **after seeing consistency**

However, in this chase we often feel behind.

Some of you may be making $10k/month while looking at my P&L and think "I NEED to do more"

Some of you I've spoken to personally making $50k/month and you think your behind.

Others of you may have just joined and still unprofitable.

At the end of the day, I know we are all here to make money and grow not only as traders but people because trading will truly expose your real self.

With this being said, let's try something new tomorrow.

Let's go into the day (all of us) without P&L on screen.

Remove your profit and loss screen from trading.

So all we see is the entry (if trade sets up) and exit.

We simply trade the chart, no emotions involved. 

If we win, fantastic

If we lose, fantastic

I think if 90% of people add this small tweak in their trading it would help tremendously.

It will show you a new perspective of trading the CHART with no bias.

If you like it, you can do it next week as well.

See y'all tomorrow. 

@everyone

author: TonyMontana
content:
Today was one of those days where every single trade I took worked and the thesis was right but timing was off.

This will happen from time to time where you can see after we traded every single trade hit profit target.

It’s important to not let that cloud your ability to trade.

Could I have entered those reclaims and make my money back.

Yes, I knew PLTR was gonna hit HOD with high prob after that 5m pullback.

But for me what’s more important:

Hoping to win my money back or following my rules every single day and letting the money come based off the process of those actions.

The answer is following my rules.

Great lesson today.

@everyone

Gameplan January 8th 

FOMC minutes today at 2:00PM EST. Market ended up closing close at LOD yesterday and this morning we have a bit pullback as well. Really what I would like to see is the QQQ reaction around 516. That is the key pivot level. SPY the key pivot level is 588. 

As for names I like #1 is AAPL if we can stay heavy underneath 242. We can look for an opening play or a hold underneath that level. This one is looking best for a move lower. 

Everything else is dependent on the QQQ. The other tech names will do what the QQQ is going to do. 

Wait for names with relative strength or weakness. Don't hold on to names for a long time. Just look for reactions around key pivot levels like PDL and PDH and gap fill areas.

author: Jdub
content:
Breakeven week for me 

Full transparency very tough past two weeks in the markets ever since the trump tariffs news

Trades setups will look good but ultimately not work out just due to the context of the overall markets. 

The markets have been very difficult due to the lack of continuation so if you are feeling down its normal. 

During these periods its best to play defense and be a lot more selective. 

Don't try and force setups or try and switch strategies. The markets are now back at aths so I expect some nice price action with a bunch of earnings and new events coming up. 

The key is to not only protect your capital its also to protect your mental capital. 

I will be getting plenty of rest this weekend and hopefully I feel better by Monday! 

Hope you guys have a great weekend! @everyone

author: MambaTrades
content:
What’s up everyone! 👋

Super excited to be here! I’ve been trading for 5 years and went all-in on futures last year.

I’m based in Southern California, and my trading name is inspired by my childhood hero, Kobe Bryant—RIP to the GOAT! 🐍

What I love about futures is the ability to manage risk effectively without battling theta decay. My system uses a 1-candle stop loss, allowing for precision and tight risk control.

If you're a newer trader or working with limited capital, futures are the best way to go. They allow you to develop a strong system and gain real experience without high costs. Once things start clicking, you can leverage prop firms and scale to $10K+ per month.

I primarily trade the 4 major indices (ES, NQ, RTY, YM) but focus mostly on ES and NQ because they offer consistent moves and high-quality risk-reward setups almost daily.

My approach is level-to-level trading based on liquidity and market structure. I’m here to share my system and processes to help you build an edge, develop strong trading habits, and master your psychology. 

Big shoutout to Jack and Tony for the opportunity—let’s crush it together! 🫡 

@here

author: MambaTrades
content:
Futures update @everyone 

Patience will be key this week. Monday and Tuesday could be slow with no red folder news. I think we will have better action Wednesday-Friday.

Every Monday have the mindset of taking 0 trades unless there is a clear setup. Worst thing you can do is start the week of in a large deficit. The plan for tomorrow is to target London high (once established), Asia high, or previous day high. If we are chopping on the hourly and 4hr it’s probably best to just sit on hands and wait till Tuesday to Friday. 

Reminder, you do not need to trade every day especially on Monday. You only need 2-4 good quality trades per week to be really successful in futures
